extracted_value: Following completion of the informed consent process and a review of Inclusion/Exclusion criteria to determine eligibility, each enrolled subject will receive a unique study identification number. Study coordinator will ask subjects relevant medical history questions. Subject demographics including age, sex, and race/ethnicity, and trimester if pregnant will also be collected at this time. The [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] Test will be performed by untrained intended use operators, e.g., nurses, physician assistants, and medical assistants. Each site will have a minimum of three (3) untrained intended users who will perform the fingerstick and each step of the testing as described in this protocol and QRI. The [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] test will be performed at the site on the same day as the date of collection for each subject enrolled. In addition, a serum sample (1-2 tubes) will be collected by a trained staff for comparator testing at a reference lab. A total of 150 subjects (at each OBGYN site, n=300) and 200 subjects (at STI site) are to be enrolled. An interim analysis may take place to ensure the predicted prevalence is met, after which a decision may be made to add sites to meet the prevalence. Once the clinical study is completed, each site will be required to perform Near LoD and reproducibility studies to assess the assay’s accuracy and precision. Sample pools will be sent to sites, de-identified, for assessment. This testing is to be integrated as part of the normal testing workflow. At the conclusion of the study, the untrained operators will be asked to fill in a usability questionnaire to evaluate the instructions for use, the clarity of the information to collect a sample, how to perform the test and read the result.

Sample Type(s)

extracted_value: Fingerstick whole blood and serum samples (comparator) from the same patient.

Statistical Methods

extracted_value: Positive and Negative Percent Agreement (PPA and NPA; aka Sensitivity and Specificity) against the comparator Focus Diagnostic HerpesSelect [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 Immunoblot [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] Test (K000238) will be estimated, along with their associated 2- sided Wilson Score with 95% Confidence Intervals. Equivocal results on the comparator device will be retested by a validated Western Blot method, and the results from that method will be used to either reclassify the comparator result as a positive or negative result, or to concur with the comparator result. Discordant results between Viro [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] and the Focus [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] Immunoblot will be resolved by retesting the remnant serum sample on a validated Western Blot method. These final results will be used in the calculation of PPA and NPA and presented as a footnote. If invalid results are obtained on the Viro [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 test, another fingerstick will be performed and the testing will be repeated on another Viro [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 test cassette. In the case of invalid results for the comparator, the leftover serum sample will be retested on the Focus [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A]1&2 [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] immunoblot test. Only valid results will be used for the analysis. The results will be analyzed for individual as well as combined study sites.

Endpoints

extracted_value: To evaluate the safety and effectiveness of the [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] and demonstrate that this device can be used to detect [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] 1&2 antibodies in fingerstick whole blood in POC CLIA waived settings.
